ocr.text: In the Inventory Turnover by Category table, the headers include InventoryTurnover, ProductCategory, and Year, with
subsequent years 2019 and 2020 detailing specific metrics. For Electronics, the turnover is 30%, with UnitsSold at 1.5M in
2019 and 1.8M in 2020, reflecting a percentage change of 20%. Clothing shows a turnover of 25%, with UnitsSold of 2.0M in
2019 and 2.2M in 2020, translating to a 15% increase. HomeGoods displays a turnover of 20%, with UnitsSold reaching 1.2M
in 2019 and 1.5M in 2020, marking a 10% growth. Lastly, Toys exhibit a turnover of 15%, with UnitsSold of 800K in 2019 and
1.0M in 2020, indicating a 5% increase.
InventoryTurnover  Percentage
ProductCategory  UnitsSold
Electronics  30%  1.5M  1.8M  20%
Clothing  25%  2.0M  2.2M  15%
HomeGoods  20%  1.2M  1.5M  10%
In the Consumer Sentiment Index table, headers comprise Year, IndexValue, Change(%), P/ERatio, along with RetailSector
metrics of CurrentRatio, Debt/Equity, MarketShare, ProfitMargin, and SalesGrowth(%). For 2019, the IndexValue is 75.5,
Change is 2.3%, CurrentRatio is 15.2, Debt/Equity is 1.5, ProfitMargin is 0.45, and SalesGrowth is 25.3%. For 2020, values
drop to IndexValue 70.2, Change -7.0%, with CurrentRatio 14.8, Debt/Equity 1.6, ProfitMargin 0.40, SalesGrowth 24.5%. The
trend continues with IndexValues of 78.0 in 2021, 82.3 in 2022, 85.1 in 2023, and 88.5 in 2024, alongside corresponding
changes and financial metrics reflecting ongoing market dynamics.
